attrs.summary:
Client selects a client context by UUID. Requires authenticated connection.
The user must have access to the client (global admin, client creator, or client_users entry).
On success, server emits setClientSuccess. On failure, server emits error with one of:
- "clientId is required" (400)
- "Unauthorized" (missing or invalid auth on socket)
- "You do not have access to this client" (403)
- "Remote connection failed: ..." (connection to agent-manager failed)

Error responses. Common message values:
- "clientId is required" (setClient missing clientId)
- "Unauthorized" (socket missing userInfo after auth middleware)
- "You do not have access to this client" (403 - setClient forbidden)
- "No client selected. Call setClient first." (forward without setClient)
- "Remote connection not established" (forward failed)
- "Remote connection failed: ..." (connection to agent-manager failed)

Forward an arbitrary event to the selected client's agents namespace. When `agentId` is provided,
the gateway automatically logs in the agent using stored credentials before forwarding the event.
To restore chat history for an agent, forward a "login" event with `agentId` (the payload is
automatically overridden with credentials from the database, triggering login and subsequent
chat history restoration). Supported events include "chat", "enhanceChat" (prompt improvement;
payload `{ message, correlationId, model? }`; response `chatEnhanceResult` unicast from
agent-manager, proxied to this socket only), "generateTicketBody" (ticket body from title;
payload `{ title, correlationId, model? }`; response `ticketBodyResult` unicast, same envelope
shape as `chatEnhanceResult`), "fileUpdate", "logout", "createTerminal", "terminalInput",
"closeTerminal", etc. The remote agent-manager gateway will process the event and may emit
response events (e.g., "chatMessage", "chatEnhanceResult", "ticketBodyResult",
"fileUpdateNotification", "terminalCreated", "terminalOutput", "terminalClosed", "containerStats")
which are automatically forwarded back to the client. The "containerStats" event payload
(defined in the agent-manager AsyncAPI) includes container status (running/stopped) and, when
running, container statistics. For autonomous ticket runs, proxied `chat` may include
`responseMode: "single" | "stream" | "sync"`, `ephemeral: true`, `continue`, and `resumeSessionSuffix`;
the controller also uses isolated suffixes (e.g. `-ticket-auto-loop`, `-ticket-auto-commit-msg`) for
implementation turns vs. post-verify conventional-commit subject generation; statistics on the controller
side record the latter under interaction kind `autonomous_ticket_commit_message`.
After a successful run, the controller also completes the workflow over HTTP by proxying agent-manager VCS
`commit` and `push` (see controller OpenAPI under `/clients/{clientId}/agents/{agentId}/vcs/...`).
agent-manager passes these to `sendMessage` and skips `agent_messages` persistence when `ephemeral` is true.
When `ephemeral` is true, chatMessage / chatEvent traffic for that turn is emitted only to the requesting
socket (not broadcast to other viewers on the same agent).
After a successful **login** (explicit `forward` with `event: "login"` or first-time auto-login before another event),
the controller may emit **ticketAutomationRunChatUpsert** (unicast) to hydrate ticket automation runs for that
`clientId` + `agentId` into the chat timeline (see `clients/ticketAutomationRunChatUpsert`). Live run updates use
the same event broadcast to room `client:{clientId}` on the **clients** namespace.
When a ticket row is created or updated (REST or internal), the controller also emits **ticketChatTicketUpsert**
to room `client:{clientId}` on **clients** (same body as `tickets/ticketUpsert`).

Controller-originated ticket automation snapshot for the chat UI (namespace **clients** only; not forwarded from
agent-manager). Aligns with OpenAPI schema `TicketAutomationRunChatEventDto`. Hydration uses `timelineAt` from
`run.startedAt`; live updates use `run.updatedAt`.

Full `TicketResponseDto` snapshot on namespace **clients** (room `client:{clientId}`), emitted whenever the
controller would emit `ticketUpsert` on namespace **tickets**, so manager/chat UIs can merge ticket metadata
without a `/tickets` socket connection.
Server notifies client that the remote connection to agent-manager has been lost
Server notifies client that reconnection to agent-manager is in progress
Server notifies client that reconnection to agent-manager was successful
Server notifies client that a reconnection attempt to agent-manager failed (but will retry)
Server notifies client that all reconnection attempts to agent-manager have failed

Select workspace for ticket board realtime (Socket.IO namespace "tickets"). Same auth and `ensureClientAccess`
rules as `clients/setClient`. Joins room `client:{clientId}`; server emits only to sockets in that room.
Errors for tickets namespace (e.g. missing clientId, Unauthorized, forbidden client, or "Unable to set client context").

Full ticket row after create/update (aligns with OpenAPI TicketResponseDto).
A single REST create with `creationTemplate: specification` emits one upsert per ticket (parent plus four subtasks).
`POST /tickets/{id}/migrate` emits one `ticketRemoved` per id on the source workspace, then one `ticketUpsert` per migrated row on the target workspace.
Ticket deleted or removed from a workspace list (e.g. workspace move from old client)
New activity row (audit trail); aligns with OpenAPI TicketActivityResponseDto
Automation settings row; aligns with OpenAPI ticket automation response

Automation run summary (may omit `steps`). During agent iterations, emissions may be throttled (about 1s per run)
in addition to `ticketAutomationRunStepAppended` for each step.
New run step row (e.g. agent_turn iteration)

Select workspace for knowledge board realtime (Socket.IO namespace "pages"). Same auth and `ensureClientAccess`
rules as `clients/setClient`. Joins room `client:{clientId}`; server emits only to sockets in that room.
Errors for pages namespace (e.g. missing clientId, Unauthorized, forbidden client, or "Unable to set client context").
Workspace-scoped signal to refresh the knowledge tree from REST.
Workspace-scoped signal to refresh affected relations from REST.
Incremental page activity row for currently open page details.

Full per-user notification state on connect (git dirty + unread per environment, client rollups, spacesHasAttention).
Unicast to the connecting socket only. Namespace **status** (STATUS_WEBSOCKET_NAMESPACE, default "status").
Client marks an environment chat as read; persists user_environment_read_state.
Client reports the environment currently in view (suppresses unread for active user).

Get available geography options for a provisioning provider

Get chat I/O records for a client

Get filter drops (aggregate or by client)
Get filter flags (aggregate or by client)

Get server types for a provider

Preview tax treatment and rates for the authenticated customer
Revalidate customer VAT ID via VIES
Get open and overdue invoices summary
Get invoice details by reference (no subscription)
Download invoice PDF by reference (no subscription)
Download void credit note PDF by reference (no subscription)
Download time report PDF by reference (no subscription)
Record usage (admin or API key only)
Bill unbilled open positions now (admin only)
List subscriptions for a user (admin only)

Download stored time report PDF (admin only)
List promotion redemptions for current user
List active promotion benefits for current user
Get customer trust score detail (admin only)
Start auto-billing payment method setup for a customer (admin)
Billing feature capabilities for admin UI (admin only)
Preview tax treatment and rates (issuer or customer)
List DATEV EXTF exports (admin only; requires BILLING_DATEV_EXPORT_ENABLED)
Trigger DATEV export for a calendar month (admin only)
List projects assigned to the authenticated customer
Delete project when no unbilled time entries
Unbilled time entry range for bill-time defaults
Bill unbilled project time in range to invoice
Generate live time report PDF (admin only)
Client sends login command with credentials (agentId or name + password)
Server acknowledges successful login. After this event, chat history is automatically restored via chatMessage events.

Client sends a chat message (requires prior login). Payload may include `responseMode` "single" | "stream" | "sync",
`ephemeral` (skip `agent_messages` persistence for the turn), `continue`, `resumeSessionSuffix`, `correlationId`, `model`.
When `ephemeral` is true, `chatMessage`, `chatEvent`, and `messageFilterResult` for that turn are emitted only to the
requesting socket (not broadcast to other clients on the same agent). Otherwise those events are broadcast to every
socket authenticated to the agent. The gateway forwards options to `sendMessage` as implemented in code.

Client requests prompt improvement in an isolated session (requires prior login). Does not persist to agent chat history or broadcast chatMessage. Result is emitted only to the requesting socket as chatEnhanceResult.
Unicast success envelope; data.success true with enhancedText, or data.success false with error (still success true on outer envelope)

Client requests ticket body text from a title in an isolated session (requires prior login). Does not persist to agent chat history. Result is emitted only to the requesting socket as ticketBodyResult (same payload shape as chatEnhanceResult).
Unicast result for generateTicketBody; same envelope as ChatEnhanceResult (enhancedText holds generated body).

Emits standardized chat line envelopes (`success` + `data` + `timestamp`). Normally broadcast to all clients
authenticated to the same agent; for a `chat` request with `ephemeral: true`, user/agent lines for that turn are
unicast to the requesting socket only. After successful login, chat history is replayed as `chatMessage` events in
chronological order to the authenticating client only.

Structured chat events (streaming deltas, tool calls/results, questions, final assistant messages). Normally broadcast to all clients authenticated to the same agent; for an ephemeral chat turn, events for that turn are emitted only to the requesting socket. Additive; does not replace chatMessage.

Filter outcome for incoming or outgoing messages. Follows the same broadcast vs ephemeral unicast rules as chatMessage: when the related `chat` request had `ephemeral: true`, results for that turn go only to the requesting socket.
Generic error messages for unauthorized or processing failures
Client sends file update notification (requires prior login). Broadcasts to all clients authenticated to the same agent.
Server broadcasts file update notifications to all clients authenticated to the same agent. Includes socket ID to identify the source of the update.
Client sends logout command to end authenticated session
Client sends create terminal command (requires prior login). Creates a new TTY session for the agent's container.
Server acknowledges successful terminal creation. Terminal session is client-specific (socket.id based).
Client sends input data to a terminal session (requires prior login). Only the socket that created the session can send input.
Server sends terminal output data to the client. Emitted whenever the terminal session produces output.
Client sends close terminal command (requires prior login). Only the socket that created the session can close it.
Server acknowledges terminal closure. Also emitted when terminal session ends (e.g., exit command).

Server broadcasts container status (running/stopped) and, when running, container statistics (CPU, memory, network, etc.) periodically to all clients authenticated to the agent. First broadcast is sent immediately after successful login, then every 15 seconds by default (CONTAINER_STATS_SCHEDULER_INTERVAL in milliseconds on the agent-manager) while clients remain authenticated. When the container is stopped, stats is null but status is always present.
Get regex filter rule by id
Create or update a workspace configuration override
Run verifier shell commands in the agent container
Get deployment configuration for an agent
Create or update deployment configuration for an agent
Delete deployment configuration for an agent
List repositories accessible with the agent's deployment configuration
List deployment runs for an agent
Get the status of a pipeline run
Get logs for a pipeline run
List jobs/steps for a pipeline run
Get logs for a specific job/step

Encryption (required for production; set for local if testing encrypted fields)

Terminal 1: billing manager (QUEUE_ROLE=all runs API, worker, and scheduler in one process)
Set QUEUE_ROLE=all, REDIS_PORT=6380 if Redis is published on the host, and auth variables
Terminal 2: billing console (Angular dev server on port 4500)
Redis (6380 when using compose host mapping)
Or built-in users (default in local Angular environment):
Encryption (set before storing sensitive provider or SSH fields)
